The data shows an easing cycle that aggressively brought rates down from >3.25% in late 2024 to 2.00% by June 2025. Since June 2025, the rate has been perfectly flat at 2.00%, suggesting the ECB has likely reached its terminal rate for this cycle or is pausing. Because central bank rate increases occur only at scheduled meetings (and even then, only when inflation flares), the likelihood of a surprise increase in the next 7 days (by Nov 16) is negligible. Over the medium term (Dec 2025 to Feb 2026), an increase remains unlikely given the preceding easing trend, but uncertainty naturally grows over time. Thus, starting from the statistical anchor, I heavily downgrade the probabilities to reflect macroeconomic realities and central bank mechanics, while increasing them toward 0.5 for the more distant horizons.

However, the sheer size of the January 2025 ruling and a possible governmental transition mean BIS may prioritize compliance enforcement rather than pushing out another major update before year-end, bringing the probability down slightly from historical base rates. Therefore, I estimate a 60% probability.

The question defines a recession strictly as two consecutive quarters of negative GDP growth initially reported within the 2025 calendar year. Therefore, I estimate a 12% probability.

There was no recession in 2023 or through the first three quarters of 2024, with US GDP growing steadily. The NBER defines a recession as a significant decline in economic activity. For a recession to have started in 2024, it would have to begin in late Q4 2024. Even if this occurred, the NBER Business Cycle Dating Committee typically takes 12-18 months to officially declare the start of a recession. Thus, for a Q4 2024 recession, an announcement by the end of 2025 is highly improbable. Given the strong economic data of 2023-2024, the likelihood of an NBER declaration of a 2023/2024 recession by the end of 2025 is exceptionally low.

Sepsis is a complex, life-threatening clinical syndrome caused by a dysregulated immune response to infection, rather than a single specific pathogen. Because it can be caused by practically any bacterial, viral, or fungal infection, developing a single 'sepsis vaccine' is scientifically unfeasible with current technology. While specific vaccines exist for common pathogens that can lead to sepsis (such as pneumococcus or meningococcus), a universal vaccine specifically for sepsis itself would represent a paradigm-shifting medical breakthrough. There are no ongoing late-stage clinical trials for a universal sepsis vaccine that could plausibly be completed, peer-reviewed, approved, and subsequently updated on Wikipedia within the near-term required forecast horizon (November 2025 to February 2026). Given this background, the probability of Wikipedia listing 'Yes' for a Sepsis vaccine is exceptionally low, with only a marginal increase over time to account for unforeseen edge cases or Wikipedia vandalism that happens to be captured on the exact resolution date.

Historically, the 1500m freestyle LC world record is broken very rarely (roughly once a decade: 2001, 2011, 2012, 2024) and almost exclusively during the summer peak season at the Olympics or World Championships. The period between November 2025 and February 2026 is the off-season for Long Course swimming (most winter meets are Short Course, e.g., National Championships and SC World Cups). The 2025 LC World Championships already concluded in July/August 2025 in Singapore. Thus, there are no major LC meets where elite swimmers would be fully tapered to challenge such a demanding record during this three-month window. The only small risks are a random national meet breakthrough or a retroactive doping invalidation, both of which are extremely unlikely (Finke's record is from over a year ago, so Olympic doping screens are mostly finalized). Taking the maximum allowed ceiling (0.95) for the nearest date and introducing slight uncertainty decay for distant dates.

Nakamura's classical FIDE rating on the November 2025 Wikipedia list is 2813. A 1% increase would require a rating gain of at least 28 points, putting him at 2841+. 

1) On 2025-11-16, the FIDE list will not have updated (updates occur on the 1st of the month), so his rating on Wikipedia will be identical to 2025-11-09 (a 0% change). The probability of a 1% increase here is limited to extreme edge cases like retroactive rating corrections or site vandalism (0.02).

2) In classical chess, the K-factor for top players is 10, meaning a player gains 10 points for a full point scored above their expected score. Gaining 28 points would require Nakamura to score at least 2.8 points MORE than expected against elite competition over the span of 1-3 months. This equates to an absurdly dominant tournament performance (e.g., scoring +7 or +8 in a 10-game tournament against 2750+ GMs). 

3) Nakamura plays classical chess infrequently, typically preferring draws and solid play rather than high variance streaks, making such an astronomical rating jump practically impossible in such a short timeframe. The probabilities are at or near the 0.02 floor, increasing slightly for the later dates due strictly to the longer time horizon and potential for unforeseen edge cases.

It is virtually impossible for a new vaccine against relapsing fever to be developed, approved, and added to the Wikipedia 'List of infectious diseases' within the 1- to 3-month forecast horizon. The question asks if a Trachoma vaccine will be developed and reflected in the Wikipedia "List of infectious diseases" by Nov 2025, Dec 2025, and Feb 2026. As of Oct 30, 2025, the Wiki list states "No". Trachoma is caused by Chlamydia trachomatis. Vaccine development for chlamydia has been historically and medically very difficult due to complex immune response requirements and the risk of immunopathology. The current global WHO strategy for trachoma elimination is "SAFE" (Surgery, Antibiotics, Facial cleanliness, Environmental control), emphasizing that no functional vaccine is available or on the immediate horizon. While early-stage clinical trials (e.g., phase 1/2) for chlamydia vaccines have occurred, phase 3 trials and regulatory approvals generally require years to complete. Given the short forecast horizons (7 days, 1 month, and 3 months), the probability of a vaccine suddenly completing clinical trials, being announced as created, and updated on Wikipedia is negligible. I estimate base probabilities are effectively zero, but apply 0.05 as the lower bound based on strict forecasting rule limits, marginally increasing over time.

FIDE updates classical rankings exactly once a month (on the 1st of each month). The Wikipedia page for FIDE rankings mirrors these official lists. As of October 31, 2025, Wikipedia shows the November 1, 2025 list with Fabiano Caruana's rating at 2795. Since Wikipedia will not be updated with officially certified monthly ratings until December 1, Caruana's rating will still be listed as 2795 on November 16. A 1% increase over 2795 corresponds to reaching an Elo rating of roughly 2823. Thus, on Nov 16, the likelihood is effectively 0 (bounded to 0.02). To reach 2823 by December 9 or February 7, Caruana would have to gain 28 rating points at an already elite level (~2800). Gains of this magnitude typically require winning an incredibly strong tournament (or multiple) with almost no losses, which is highly improbable in a single month and remains very unlikely over a 3-month period. Forecasting a slow, widening uncertainty (0.02 -> 0.03 -> 0.06) as time increases.

On 2025-11-09, Wei Yi's FIDE rating according to the Wikipedia page will be 2753 (based on the November 1, 2025 update). For the rating to be at least 1% higher, it would need to increase by roughly 28 points, reaching at least 2781. 
For the first resolution date (2025-11-16), FIDE rankings update monthly, meaning the November 1 list will still be active and Wikipedia's page will almost certainly still show 2753. Since 2753 is not at least 1% higher than 2753, it is effectively impossible for this to resolve YES. I assign the minimum allowable probability of 0.05.
For the second resolution date (2025-12-09), a jump of 28 points in a single month at the Super GM level (2750+ Elo) requires a historically dominant classical performance (approximately +6 or +7 against elite opposition). This is extremely rare, so the probability remains very low at 0.06.
For the third resolution date (2026-02-07), three FIDE lists will have passed, giving him slightly more time (including potentially playing at Tata Steel). However, gaining 28 points within 3 months to reach 2781 is still a massive undertaking that would likely place him in the world top 5, which is highly improbable though slightly more possible than in one month. I assign 0.09.

Enterobiasis (pinworm infection) is generally a mild, easily treatable parasitic infection (usually with medications like albendazole or mebendazole). There are no approved vaccines for enterobiasis in humans, and virtually no active research into developing one because the disease burden does not justify the immense cost of vaccine development, especially when cheap and effective treatments already exist. Since there are no scientific or clinical trials developing a pinworm vaccine, it is essentially impossible for a vaccine to be successfully developed, approved, and listed on the Wikipedia page for infectious diseases by any of the resolution dates provided (all within early 2026). The only scenario where the page might list a vaccine is through temporary vandalism, which is statistically rare to land precisely on the resolution date and survive without being reverted. Thus, the probability remains at the 2% floor for all periods.

None of the current candidates are at the stage of imminent approval in late 2025. Therefore, the probability of the Wikipedia page being updated to reflect a 'Yes' for a created vaccine is very low across all three dates, scaling slightly upward over time to account for unforeseen fast-tracked approvals or edit disputes.
